statistics.trappedVessels: 500+ oil/gas tankers, 500+ container ships, 6 cruise ships
statistics.note: Iran approved transit for 8 nations for selective transit; Pakistan secured agreement for 20 ships/day (2 daily under Pakistani flag). All major commercial carriers still suspended. 27-day blockade with first crack via Islamabad summit.

timeline.event: Iran begins deploying naval mines in Strait of Hormuz
IRGC deploys anti-ship missiles along Iranian coastline
Full blockade declared. War-risk insurance pulled by Lloyd's of London.
4 commercial vessels struck by mines/missiles. 150+ vessels at anchor.
1 oil tanker sunk. All major shipping companies halt Persian Gulf operations.
US Navy minesweeping operations begin. No commercial transit.
Only 9 vessels total crossed strait this week. Dry bulk transits down 91%. ~280 bulkers trapped. IRGC drones hit tankers Prima and Louise P.
Tehran oil depots bombed — fuel distribution halted. Brent crude +27% for the week. US Navy still not escorting. IRGC vows 6+ months capability.

Strait effectively closed — zero transits for 48+ hours. 310+ vessels at anchor. 8 commercial vessels damaged total, 2 sunk. Brent crude hits $119.50 intraday before settling at $103.47. US minesweeping makes minimal progress. IRGC deploys additional fast attack boats.

Day 11 — Only 13 vessels/day average; March 8 saw only 2 outbound transits. 170+ containerships trapped, 150+ tankers at anchor. Insurance withdrawn by all major providers. VLCC charter rates surge from $50K to $400K/day. Brent crude holds above $110.

Day 12 — 3 more ships attacked (total 14 damaged/sunk since war began). Shipping traffic down 95% from pre-war. 330+ vessels at anchor. IEA approves record 400M barrel emergency reserve release. Brent rebounds +3.4% to $90.77. Iran deploys Khoramshahr super-heavy missiles.

Day 13 — Two more tankers hit in Iraqi waters (total 16 damaged/sunk). 280+ bulk carriers stranded. Dry bulk trade down 91%. Brent crosses $100. IEA 400M barrel release across 32 countries (US contributing 172M from SPR) fails to contain surge. Mojtaba Khamenei vows Hormuz stays closed.

No commercial transits in either direction for 24 hours (Bloomberg). Iran considering yuan-only access. 2 Indian LPG carriers allowed under Indian Navy escort — rare exception. Hegseth: 'no clear evidence' of new mines but strait 'won't remain contested.'

Day 14: Trump strikes Kharg Island military targets but spares oil. Iran threatens oil companies with US connections. IRGC hits US Embassy Baghdad. 16+ vessels attacked in strait since war began. Iran considering yuan-only passage proposal.

Day 15: Fujairah port oil operations suspended after drone attack. IRGC threatens UAE cities. 20+ commercial vessels attacked since March 1. Hormuz remains effectively closed. 500+ oil/gas tankers and 500+ container ships stranded.

Iran selectively reopens Hormuz — allows Turkish cargo ship, 2 Indian LNG carriers, Saudi oil tanker. Western vessels still prohibited. 150+ ships anchored outside. US 'not ready' to escort oil ships through (Energy Sec. Wright).
timeline.note: Separate from military events same day — this entry covers selective reopening policy

timeline.event: Day 16: IRGC warns any vessel linked to US/Israel is 'legitimate target,' continues to threaten UAE ports. Turkey and India getting selective passage through strait. Western-flagged vessels still prohibited.

Day 16 PM: Iranian supertanker pushes through Hormuz bound for China. Trump calls for naval coalition — no countries publicly agree. Major carriers still suspended. Selective passage continues for Turkey, India, Saudi ships.
timeline.note: Afternoon update — Iranian supertanker transit and coalition proposal

timeline.event: Iran issues evacuation warnings for specific areas of Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Doha ports; Fujairah oil loading resumes after Saturday fire; two Indian LPG tankers cross with Iran's permission
Trump urges China and allies to send warships; coalition expected to be announced 'this week' (WSJ). No countries publicly commit yet
Dubai airport fuel tank struck by Iranian drone — flights suspended 7+ hours. Fujairah oil hub struck again, oil loading suspended. 300+ ships stranded in Gulf
EU's Kallas proposes Black Sea grain deal-style Hormuz arrangement via UN. Japan PM rules out sending warships. WSJ: coalition announcement imminent

Zero commercial vessel transits recorded on March 14 — first complete stoppage in history. ~400 vessels stuck in surrounding waters. Only shadow fleet and Chinese-linked ships making sporadic crossings.

Iran selectively allowing passage: Pakistan, India, Turkey confirmed. China, France, Italy negotiating. Fewer than 5 ships/day vs 138 historical average. EU Kallas proposes 'Black Sea model' oil corridor with UN.

Treasury Secretary Bessent says US will allow Iranian oil tankers through Hormuz. WSJ reports US building multinational naval escort coalition. Oil drops 4-6% on the news. Pentagon exploring diplomatic off-ramps.

Day 18: Israel kills Ali Larijani. Bessent Hormuz tanker policy still in effect but no commercial vessels transit. Fujairah oil zone struck for 3rd time. Pakistan tanker transited with Iran permission. 340+ vessels stranded.
Iran sends 11.7M barrels to China via shadow fleet despite blockade. Only Chinese-linked, Pakistani, and Indian vessels getting selective passage. Western commercial shipping remains fully blocked.
Iran strikes UAE Shah gas field (20% of UAE gas supply), Fujairah oil terminal hit 3rd time in 4 days
Kuwait-flagged tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah struck near Fujairah. Iraq begins talks with Iran to negotiate ship passage. South Korea reports 26 vessels and 183 crew stranded.

Day 19: IMO emergency 2-day meeting in London — 40-member council may vote on safe maritime corridor resolution. Only 21 tankers total have transited Hormuz since war began — traffic down 97%. Bulk carriers finding new route through Iranian territorial waters. India attempting to escort 6 vessels through.

Iran launched fresh attacks across Persian Gulf on Gulf neighbors. Some ships spoofing transponders as Chinese-flagged to gain passage. Liberian-flagged bulk carrier broadcast destination as 'CHINA OWNER_ALL CREW' to transit.
IMO talks on safe maritime corridor begin; Iran strikes all 6 GCC energy sites. Brent surges past $119 intraday.

Six Western allies issue joint statement on safe passage; UK sends military planners to CENTCOM. 21 total tanker transits since war began vs 100+/day pre-war. Iran allowing select friendly-nation ships.

7-nation Hormuz coalition formed (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Canada). IMO begins negotiations for humanitarian corridor to free ~20,000 stranded seafarers. Dubai crude hits record $166.80/barrel. QatarEnergy declares force majeure on LNG — 17% capacity offline 3-5 years.
Day 21 (Nowruz): Strait remains closed (Day 18). Israel pounds Tehran. Bessent signals unsanctioning 140M barrels Iranian crude. Kuwait refinery struck. Brent $110.28.

Day 18: Iran developing selective 'vetting system' for Hormuz transit — India, China, Turkey, Pakistan, Malaysia negotiating case-by-case passage. At least 9 ships have transited Iranian territorial waters corridor; one tanker reported paying $2M passage fee. ~8 non-Iranian vessels detected via AIS (nearly double recent days). Iran still effectively blocking Western shipping.

Day 22: Blockade Day 19. Iran refuses to negotiate Hormuz reopening while under attack. Trump calls NATO 'COWARDS' for not sending troops to open strait. DIA assessment: Iran capable of 1–6 month blockade. 21 total tankers have crossed via Iranian territorial waters corridor since war began vs 100+/day pre-war. UK authorizes Diego Garcia for US Hormuz strike operations.

Day 22 Evening: Houthis formally threaten to close Bab el-Mandeb Strait — 'Hour Zero' readiness declared by military official Abed al-Thawr. Shipping giants Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, CMA CGM already suspended Trans-Suez sailings. A double chokepoint scenario (Hormuz + Bab el-Mandeb) would affect ~30% of global seaborne trade. Iran strikes Dimona and Arad, demonstrating continued missile capability on Day 22. Trump calls NATO 'cowards' for not protecting Hormuz.
Trump issues 48-hour ultimatum: Open Hormuz or face strikes on Iran's power plants. Iran counter-threatens Gulf desalination infrastructure. Deadline: March 23 7:44 PM ET.

Day 23: Trump 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum — deadline Monday March 23 7:44 PM ET (~20 hrs remain). Iran rejects compliance; IRGC releases 'Say goodbye to electricity' Gulf energy targeting map. Iran selectively granting passage: Japan approved (joining China, India, Pakistan); IRGC developing formal vetting/registration system requiring ship ownership and cargo data. At least one tanker paid $2M for transit rights. Iran fires 8th missile salvo at Israel. 40,000+ seafarers stranded. Zero commercial transits.

Day 23 Evening: US drops GBU-72 bunker buster bombs on hardened Iranian anti-ship missile bunkers near strait — CENTCOM Admiral Cooper: 8,000+ targets struck total, 130 vessels destroyed, 'largest naval elimination since WWII.' Trump 48-hr ultimatum (expires March 23 7:44 PM ET) met with full Iranian defiance — IRGC threatens 'complete closure' if power plants struck. Cluster munitions hit 7+ central Israel sites including Tel Aviv (15 wounded). Iran firing on Lebanon territory (first confirmed instance).
NATO Sec-Gen Rutte announces 22 nations joining coalition to reopen Hormuz; Saudi East-West Pipeline bypass to Yanbu Red Sea port declared operational as alternate route
Trump's 48-hour ultimatum expires — deadline Monday evening PDT. IRGC restates complete closure threat if power plants struck. 22 NATO nations mobilizing for Hormuz escort operation.

Trump announces 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes citing 'productive talks' — effectively extending Hormuz deadline to March 28. Iran's Defense Council threatens to mine Persian Gulf if coastline/islands attacked. First Iraqi supertanker (2M barrels) crosses strait with AIS signal off using Iran's permission system. Brent crude falls 10%+ from $112 highs to ~$101 on ceasefire diplomacy hopes.

Supertanker Omega Trader (Mitsui OSK) successfully delivers 2M barrels Iraqi crude to Mumbai via Hormuz-Iranian corridor — first Iraqi oil shipment completing a Hormuz transit since blockade began. AIS signal switched off during transit. Iran developing formal vetting/registration system; at least 9 ships have now used Iranian territorial waters corridor. One tanker reportedly paid $2M for transit rights.

Day 25 of war (Hormuz blockade day 22): Iran operating selective vetting system — allowing 9+ ships through for fees of up to $2M per transit. Japan, India, Pakistan, Iraq, Malaysia, China negotiating bilateral passage. Trump extended Hormuz ultimatum after announcing 5-day energy-strike pause citing 'productive talks'. CENTCOM Adm. Cooper confirms planning weeks-long operation to force full reopening. Status: effectively closed for non-vetted vessels.

Day 26 of war (Hormuz blockade day 23): Iran formally rejects US 15-point peace plan including Hormuz access provision — demands instead international recognition of its 'authority' over the strait as a counter-condition. Iran's UN ambassador said 'non-hostile vessels' may pass with Iranian coordination — conditional passage only, not reopening. Philippines declares national State of Energy Emergency (first country globally) citing 98% Gulf oil dependence. ~2,000 vessels and 20,000 seafarers remain stranded. Japan begins largest-ever 30-day oil reserve release (part of IEA 400M barrel effort). Brent volatile: $104.49 early AM, dropped to ~$99 on Trump peace-talks announcement.

Day 27 of war (Hormuz blockade day 24): IRGC Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri — the architect of the Hormuz blockade — killed by Israel in Bandar Abbas strike. Iran's shift to 'selective blockade' policy (announced March 25, formally allowing 'non-hostile' ships) continues — Trump revealed Iran let 8-10 tankers through as 'goodwill gesture.' Fewer than 10 AIS transits/day vs 138 pre-war. Brent spiked to $105.85 after Iran definitively rejected US 15-point peace plan. UAE struck by Iranian ballistic missiles — 2 killed in Abu Dhabi by debris. Iran fortifying Kharg Island with mines/MANPADs amid US ground operation planning. Iran Parliament Speaker warns of 'vital infrastructure' attacks on any regional country aiding US Kharg seizure. Spain becomes first European nation approved for Hormuz transit — Iran classifies Spain 'non-hostile' after Spain banned US forces from using its bases for Iran strikes. Malaysia also added. Trump extends energy-strike pause to April 6. Brent surges to $107.99-$109. S&P -1.74% worst day of war.

Day 28 (Hormuz blockade Day 25): IRGC Wave 83 targets all four major US Gulf bases simultaneously (Al-Dhafra UAE, Al-Udeidi Saudi, Ali Al-Salem Kuwait, Sheikh Isa Bahrain). IAF destroys Yazd naval missile and sea mine production facility — IDF says most central site for Iran's naval arms production. Pentagon weighing 10,000 additional ground troops including 82nd Airborne; Kharg Island seizure option remains active. CENTCOM confirms 92% of Iran's large naval ships eliminated. Brent $107.99 (+4.1%), WTI $94.48 (+4.6%). Tangsiri's replacement not yet named — IRGC Navy in transition. Selective blockade continues: Spain, Malaysia, China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan approved for transit; fewer than 10 AIS transits per day vs 138 pre-war.

Day 28 (Hormuz blockade Day 25): Iran turns back 3 Chinese ships (COSCO CSCL Indian Ocean, CSCL Arctic Ocean, Marshall Islands-flagged Lotus Rising) near Larak Island — IRGC confirms strait shut to unauthorized vessels. Brent surges to $112.57/bbl (highest since 2022), WTI briefly crosses $100 for first time this conflict. Macquarie warns $200 oil (40% probability) if Hormuz stays shut through June. Iran accepts UN request for humanitarian aid and agricultural shipping corridor. Only 21 tankers transited since war began vs 100+/day pre-war. 150+ ships anchored outside strait. Trump's extended Hormuz reopening deadline: April 7.

Blockade Day 26: Iran attacks Kuwait Airport radar (significantly damaged), Salalah Port Oman (1 injured, crane destroyed, Maersk suspends 48h), UAE industrial zone (5 injured) — simultaneous 3-nation Gulf strike; Pakistan announces Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships transit (2/day) as part of Islamabad mediation; Houthis fire first missile at Israel from Yemen threatening Bab al-Mandeb closure; Islamabad peace summit opens with Saudi/Turkey/Egypt FMs

Islamabad summit: Iran agrees to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships per day through Hormuz (2 daily rate). First partial opening of blockade in 27 days. Trump extends full-reopening deadline to April 6.

impactedGoods.currentFlow: ~0 bbl/day (selective passage for Japan, China, India, Pakistan)
impactedGoods.detail: 20% of global supply cut off — Day 24 of blockade. Iran shifted to 'selective blockade' policy on March 25 — 'non-hostile ships' may transit with Iran's approval; Japan explicitly approved. Still fewer than 10 AIS-visible transits/day (vs 138 pre-war). IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri — the architect of the blockade — was killed by Israel on March 26. His elimination may affect IRGC naval command coordination but blockade infrastructure (mines, missiles, vetting system) remains in place. 3,200+ ships trapped, 40,000+ seafarers stranded. VLCC charter rates at $800K/day.
Qatar's LNG export facilities damaged. 17 Iranian ballistic missiles intercepted over Doha on March 8. European gas prices at record levels.

Jet fuel, diesel, gasoline exports halted. US gas at $3.981/gal (AAA March 26), up 25.2% from pre-war $3.18. Just $0.019 from $4.00 milestone. California: $5.84/gal. Diesel at $5.29 (+33.4%). Iran shifted to selective blockade — 'non-hostile' ships may transit. Philippines declared national State of Energy Emergency (March 24) — first country globally.
Fertilizer and plastics supply chain severely disrupted. Food commodity prices surging 30-50% above pre-war levels.
